TextENGNew: The interior of the Sonozaki family's office had also become a shelter for local residents.
Everywhere you looked, there were civilians wrapped in blankets, lying on the floor.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: "The Sonozaki family exists because of its community. Under Mother's orders, we've been taking in displaced people since the chaos first broke out."
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: "Displaced people?"
"People like Une-chan. Folks who were here for work or pleasure -- anyone from outside Okinomiya who got caught up in the turmoil."
"Those self-proclaimed vigilante groups were targeting such outsiders first. We could have fought them, but Akane-san instructed us to avoid that."
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: So instead, they took in as many displaced people as they could and erected barricades to cut off contact with the vigilante mobs, preventing further conflict.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: To the vigilantes, every outsider was an infected person. If they discovered this many outsiders were being harbored here, there would be hell to pay.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: "...Is it safe here?"

"I can guarantee this is the safest place in Okinomiya. The community hall was the biggest shelter, but it seems to have collapsed. So right now, this is likely the most secure refuge in the entire city."
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: "Collapsed?"
"Too many cooks in the kitchen, as they say. A power struggle over leadership escalated into actual killing. ...It's a terrifying situation."
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: "I see. ...Shion's group had strong leadership from the start, so discipline held and nobody surrendered their heart to the demon."
changeReason: New translation specifies 'Shion's group' where old leaves it implicit.

TextENGNew: "Impressive. So are the police still functioning?"
"The officers are scattered across the city dealing with riots everywhere, ...and rumors started spreading that they were harboring infected people..."
"This situation exceeds anything the police were prepared for. There's no way they can handle it."
"That said, Tomitake-san was really something back there! Where on earth did you get such a fine weapon?"
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: "Oh, I just happened to find it lying around. Hahahahaha...!!"
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: "That's not the sort of thing an amateur could handle. ...You're quite the enigma."
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: "...Rika? ...Rika?"
"What's wrong? Wait -- where's Rika-chan? And Une-chan is missing too."
"I saw the two of them talking over there a moment ago."
"What is it, Hanyuu? What about this girl?"
"...Be quiet. I'm reaching out to her right now."
"What do you mean? ...What exactly is this girl, Une?"
A sound like a tuning fork began resonating inside Rika's head.
It shifted pitch after pitch, as though searching for the right frequency.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ...Rika understood. This was a conversation between beings beyond humanity.
It was like a radio.
They were probing for a wavelength on which they could communicate.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Then... could this girl called Une actually be...?
"...I am Hanyuu, leader of the horned people. ...Who are you?"
At that moment, the two tuning-fork tones resonated in harmony.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: A dazzling light swallowed everything...
When the light faded, Rika, Hanyuu, and Une were floating in the world of inhuman beings.
In the eyes of the girl who called herself Une, the same light that Hanyuu occasionally showed was shining.
She was certain now. This girl... was an existence on the same plane as herself and Hanyuu. ...Something not of this world.
changeReason: Narration shifted from first-person to third-person; adds 'not of this world' from JPN.

TextENGNew: Hanyuu gazed steadily into Une's eyes, waiting for her answer...
"I... am Une. ...I'm... scared..."
"There is nothing to fear. I have no intention of fighting anyone. Where do your people hail from?"
From that point on, they communicated not with words but through something resembling telepathy.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Various images flooded into Rika's mind one after another, as though she were being forced into a waking dream.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: An ocean of stars in the cosmos. ...Beyond them, the blue Earth, shining.
Une had apparently arrived on Earth only very recently.
Like Hanyuu's people before her, she had finally reached solid ground after an immeasurably long journey.
"...I see... After such a long journey, you have finally..."
"I... froze in the pitch-black sea... and at last, I arrived... ...And yet..."
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Rika finally realized. ...She was frightened.
She was anxious about whether she could survive safely on this land she had finally reached.
And who could blame her? Even from the perspective of a non-human being, the current chaos in Okinomiya was terrifying.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: A hellscape of violence, wrought by people driven mad by eta-173.
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: After drifting through the frozen void of space for so long, she had at last reached land... only to be greeted by this unspeakable horror.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Next, Hanyuu transmitted her own knowledge to Une in the form of images.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: For a while, images from both sides streamed through Rika's mind like a television rapidly flipping channels...
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Gradually, they came to understand each other's origins, circumstances, and present situations.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Rika, too, began to vaguely comprehend as the alien concepts were rendered into human language.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: First, she confirmed that Une, like Hanyuu and Tamura, was a non-human entity.
She was one of the inhuman people from space who dwelled within humans, just as humans lived upon the Earth.
She had reached this planet only recently. ...Though 'recently' was by the reckoning of an inhuman being -- in human terms, it had been several centuries.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Her body had only just acclimated to Earth, and she had just regained her self-awareness.
...She had been little more than a seed while drifting through space, only regaining consciousness after taking root in the planet's soil.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Rika also came to understand the special nature of Une's people.
The characteristic of Hanyuu's horned people was... that when they grew unstable, they would be driven to paranoia, and their hearts would be taken over by that demon.

......This was a disadvantage when it came to coexisting with humanity, and it was a problem that both Hanyuu and Tamura acknowledged about the horned people. Takano was the one who took an interest in that problem.
changeReason: New adds sentence about Takano taking interest in the problem, not present in old.

TextENGNew: That very problem was what had drawn Takano's interest.
In Une's case, her people carried no such drawback. They were far better adapted to coexisting with humanity and posed no risk to the lands they inhabited.
``So unlike you, they're the type that Tamura would have actually welcomed.``
``...There is no way Tamurahime-no-Mikoto would welcome any new arrivals. ...To her, both Une and I are invaders to be expelled.``
Une's people possessed a 'strength' nearly equal to Hanyuu's.
``Strength? ...The image flooding into my head is the strength of a plant's roots.``
``Fragile flowers blooming beautifully in a garden will sometimes be overtaken by wildflowers with stronger roots. ...Our strength is the strength of roots.``
In Hanyuu's world, their peoples were better understood through the concept of plants than animals.
...Indeed. Even in the world of plants, there was survival of the fittest.
Hanyuu and Une had very powerful survival instincts. Their peoples were hardy species with deep, strong roots.
``So what about Tamura's people? ...The ones that view you as an enemy?``
``Her people are far weaker than mine. ...But that is no one's fault, nor is it destiny. It is simply a matter of species.``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: To these inhuman beings, the Earth was a vast flower bed.
They were all trying to sow their seeds and let their species flourish.
In the distant past, Tamura's people had been the only flowers blooming in the flower bed of Earth.
Then Hanyuu's species arrived, and with their vigorous reproductive ability, they quickly crowded out Tamura's people. Tamura's species withered as a result.
...So of course, from Tamurahime-no-Mikoto's perspective, they were invaders.
On top of that, Hanyuu's species carried the risk of destroying the flower bed entirely if they grew unstable.
Nothing could survive if the flower bed itself was destroyed.
That is why Hanyuu's people agreed to confine themselves to Hinamizawa -- the flower bed best suited to them -- and sealed their roots from spreading further.
And now, Une -- a species with equal strength -- had once again entered the flower bed.
Fortunately, Une's people would not harm the flower bed.
However, they possessed the root strength to easily displace Tamura's people...
``...We have no such intentions... We just want a small patch of land for ourselves...``
``I understand how you feel. ...I support you in this as well. As one who has also crossed the ocean of stars, I will never reject a fellow traveler.``
``...In that case, she's arrived at the worst possible time. With Hanyuu's people running rampant, it's hard to imagine Tamura welcoming another species.``
``...I am... scared... ...We simply want to bloom quietly, in peace...``
``Is there no land beyond Tamura's reach?``
``Nearly all of Japan belongs to Tamura's people. ...Even if she left Japan, there are similar beings ruling other lands as well. There is truly nowhere left for her.``
``So the Earth is like a full apartment building with no vacancies...``

``...But I believe Tamurahime-no-Mikoto's people are claiming too much territory. We must respect those who were here first, but the land should be shared. Tamura is being selfish by monopolizing it, au.``
``Why don't you just go all Sengoku Era and fight it out? Survival of the fittest is the law of nature, isn't it?``
``Because that would lead to something catastrophic. Our flower bed and this very planet would meet their end.``
``...Come to think of it, you and Une are roughly equal in strength. What would happen if you two fought?``
``I-I don't want to fight...``
``I don't want to fight either, and there would be no point. Because our strength is identical, we are immune to each other. Au.``
``Meaning?``
``In our case, the flower bed goes to whichever one takes root first. Au.``
``I don't want to fight...``
``So you're saying that the people of Hinamizawa can't be infected by Une, right?``
``That's correct.``
``And likewise, humans carrying Une's parasite can't develop Hinamizawa Syndrome.``
``That would be correct.``
I think I may have figured out how to identify the aliens.
Anyway, something is nagging at me... This means...
``Hanyuu. ...What were we trying to do, again?``
``Au? Did you hit your head on tofu or something?``
``We can't let Takano run wild, right? She's trying to spread eta-173 across the world. So our mission is to stop that -- correct?``
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: ``Yes, that's right... But what about it?``
``You're an idiot! A colossal idiot! I can't believe it! It's two birds with one stone!``
``...I don't really understand...``
``We just need to forget about Tamura and help this girl live freely on our planet! The humans carrying Une's roots can't be infected by eta-173, can they?``
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: The two clueless aliens tilted their heads in unison.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: It all makes sense now! All the pieces are in place!
I know the identity of Takano's 'Devil'! This is what we have to do!
``We just need to spread Une throughout the people of Japan before Takano can! If we do, no one will be infected even if Takano scatters η173 everywhere!``
Well, completely wiping out Tamura's people would be a bit much, so for now, how about covering just the entire prefecture to block Takano's range of activity?
There are forty-seven prefectures. Tamurahime-no-Mikoto can surely spare one.
changeReason: Different numbers: old has {'47'}, new has set().

TextENGNew: Once we surround Takano's forces with Une, she won't be able to spread the infection any further.
We'll seal off the spread of eta-173 and prevent any more damage!
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: Now I understand! From Takano's perspective, that outcome would be fatal.
Une has to be the true identity of the 'Devil' she mentioned to Tomitake.
That's the only explanation that makes sense.
If Une's infection could get ahead of η173, Takano's ambitions would all come to nothing!
changeReason: Old includes detail about Une and η173 being evenly matched; new condenses to a shorter statement.

TextENGNew: ``So there you have it!``
``Oh... Ohh! That's a brilliant idea, Rika!!``
``...I-I think I kind of understand, maybe...?``
``This is an incredible plan! Une gets to live on our planet, we can prevent any more tragedy, and we can drive Tamura out of this prefecture -- three birds with one stone!!``
changeReason: Old says 'two birds with one stone'; new says 'three birds with one stone' - counts differ.

TextENGNew: If Une occupied the entire prefecture, Tamura's people would be driven out of it.
That would mean they'd never encounter Tamurahime-no-Mikoto again unless they left the prefecture, and they'd never have to endure her headache-inducing powers again.
For the first time in a thousand years, they could strike a blow against the 'god' who had claimed all of Japan as her own and bullied them relentlessly.
Hanyuu was dancing with joy. She must truly despise Tamura.
...Well, given Tamura's arrogant attitude and condescending posturing, even Rika wanted to take a swing at her.
``...What should I do...?``
``There's nothing we can do about Okinomiya. This city's already infected with eta-173. So you need to leave this city, find an uninfected area, and start spreading your roots.``
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: ``...That's right.``
``In that case, what should we do...?``
According to Tomitake, all means of transit out of the city had been cut off to prevent the spread of infection.
In other words, they couldn't get Une out of eta-173's infected region.
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: They would need to escape the city before Takano and spread Une's people.
changeReason: Narration shifted from first-person ('We would') to third-person ('They would need').

TextENGNew: They should probably consult Tomitake and gain his support.
Since Tomitake understood parasitic neuroviruses, he should be able to grasp their situation.
Even if he rejected the idea of spreading Une, the information could lead to the development of a new vaccine.
Rika called Tomitake over and explained the situation.
Naturally, getting him to understand took until dawn.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: But in the end, he did.
``Fascinating. All right. ...We'll need to defer the decision to Tokyo, but her existence is a big deal. If Une-chan is willing to cooperate, we can prepare comfortable accommodations for her.``
``I-I wouldn't mind being comfortable...``
``Tomitake is a good man. I'm sure he'll take care of you, Une.``
``...I'm so happy...``
``The end of this tragedy is finally within our sights...``
``Well, we can't declare victory until we get Une out of the city, ...but at least one problem is solved for now. That's enough.``
The Sonozaki family's office was the safest and most heavily guarded location in Okinomiya.
It had food, resources, weapons -- everything. Tomitake understood the situation and was cooperative. Une, the key person, was cooperative as well.
In chess terms, they were only a few moves from checkmate. Now was the perfect time for their side to regroup.
...Unless the Joker -- Takano -- caused some kind of upheaval.
``Plus... there's that insufferable Tamurahime-no-Whatever.``
``...She is unlikely to welcome what we're attempting.``
It was then that they heard the ringing of a tuning fork, and brilliant white light engulfed both Rika and Hanyuu.
...She was here. Tamurahime-no-Mikoto.
``Speak of the devil.``
``...How dare you, leader of the horned people! Truly, you have been plotting to defile our lands with more tainted roots!``
``We will welcome our new friend, Une. But that does not mean we intend to seize all of your land.``
``Silence, tainted one! Know that we shall never permit your conspiracy to corrupt our land!``

``Listen, there's a villain named Takano, and she's plotting to spread what you call the horned people across all of Japan -- no, the entire world! If she does, your people will be exterminated, you know?``

``And there's nothing you can do to stop it, even if you stand on your head. If you cede a little land to Une, then Une gets to live on this planet, you don't lose any more territory, and Hanyuu never has to see your face again -- everybody wins.``
``Be silent, child of man. Know that you are not permitted to speak your tainted words.``
``Owwww... Stop hitting me with that headache power...``
``She has always been stubborn and refuses to listen. Nothing you say will ever reach her.``
``You are the one who never listens! You tainted people, you horned people! My fury has not subsided, not in a thousand years of your insolence!``
``I could say the same to you! My rage hasn't faded either, not after a thousand years of your harassment!``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Rika didn't know what history those two shared, but if their grudges had festered for a millennium, reconciliation was clearly not in the cards.

TextENGNew: Apparently this was a feud beyond the comprehension of humans, who live for a mere century.

TextENGNew: Well, that's just how gods are. Humans worship them of their own accord, yet the gods themselves are petty enough to carry grudges for a thousand years.

TextENGNew: ``I don't care about the fight between you two. Tamura-no-Whatever, if you don't want to listen, fine. But this is the only way to stop the tragedy in the human world!``
``You wouldn't understand anything I said anyway, foolish priestess of the horned people. Know that I shall use my own methods to put a stop to your schemes!``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``You're a god and all, but you really tick me off. Maybe we shouldn't hold back at just one prefecture -- why not take the whole Chubu region?``

TextENGNew: ``I will not allow it! Know that I shall never permit you to corrupt the land of my people again!!``
There was the sound of glass shattering, followed by a furious, bellowing roar.
What happened?! Given the timing, it was obvious Tamura had done something.
Yet Tamurahime-no-Mikoto was an inhuman being, like Hanyuu.
She shouldn't have been able to interfere directly with the human world...
``T-Takano-san?!``
``Hello, Jirou-san. I had faith we would meet again. Heehee.``
Takano had smashed through the window and burst into the office.
The whole spectacle was as dramatic as a scene from an action film, and the men were all stunned...

TextENGNew: Even Tomitake couldn't help but stand there dumbstruck.
She had always been unpredictable, but that was in personality. She'd never shown physical prowess like this before, let alone the ability to smash through glass and leap inside.

TextENGNew: The men of the Sonozaki family could tell from her aggressive smile that she was hostile.

Takano, however, was different. She knew exactly what needed to be done.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Tomitake quickly understood as well. ...Her target was Une.
If Une's infection covered Japan first, η173 would become useless. There would be nothing more Takano could do.
But since she already knew that, she was targeting Une directly.
Searching for her hiding place somewhere in Okinomiya was inefficient. But when the Sonozaki group started sheltering people, Takano realized something.
All she had to do was wait, and Une would come to her!
``I only have business with that girl, Une. ...Would you be so kind as to bring her out?``
``I have no idea what you're talking about.``
``There's no need to play dumb. I've been watching this entire time, after all. Heeheehee...``
``...I won't let you. We're going to contact Tokyo.``
``Pardon the interruption! Welcome to the Sonozaki family's office, Miyo-san!``
``My, my, quite the welcoming committee.``
Shion appeared with a contingent of tough-looking men in tow.
It went without saying that Takano was an uninvited guest.
``You're a formidable woman, but I think you've overstepped your bounds.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``I think you're about to learn just how formidable I can be.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Takano seized a man's head in each hand and slammed them against opposite walls.
Where did such monstrous strength come from that slender frame?! No -- even a musclebound giant couldn't do something like that!
The physics simply didn't add up. Even if her slender body harbored inhuman strength, she couldn't possibly generate enough leverage to hurl grown men with such force.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Shion, Kasai, and the other men watched in shock, frozen in place before Takano's otherworldly display of power.
``Shion-chan, you should pay attention... or your head is going to end up splattered across the wall.``
Shion's stunned eyes met Takano's, and for once, genuine fear overtook her.
She couldn't be blamed. When a human witnesses something beyond comprehension, they are utterly paralyzed.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Takano's arms looked like massive bear paws to her... and before she could even blink, those paws were swinging down toward her.
``Look out!!!``
As Kasai shoved Shion out of the way, he caught Takano's swinging arm and used her own momentum to pin her down.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``What a frightening woman... Where on earth did you obtain such strength...?``
``Kasai! Nice work!``
He had a solid lock on her joints and pressed her firmly into the ground. No matter how much raw strength she had, she shouldn't be able to escape a properly applied joint lock.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Takano squirmed in an effort to break free, but she just looked like a pathetic insect pinned on its back.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``I'd advise you to stop resisting. Kasai is a scary man, and if you struggle too much, he might accidentally break something.``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``I won't unless it's necessary... but I'm no gentleman, so forgive me if it comes to that.``
``Oh my, I really can't move. Heehee...``
With her joints locked so securely, the pain should have been enough to twist her face in agony.
Yet Takano remained collected, seemingly amused by her own inability to move.
``Is this the full extent? Of the power you're granting me...? ...It's not, is it? Hee... Heeheeheehee!!``
``Nnngh! ...Nngoh...!``
Takano used her other arm... and with that single arm alone, lifted both herself and her captor, Kasai, clean off the ground.
It was like watching some kind of circus act.
No. Takano's grotesque, inhuman strength allowed her, with just one arm, to lift both Kasai and herself into the air.
Even Kasai couldn't help but be shaken.
In the next instant, he lost his grip, and she spun Kasai like a ragdoll before hurling him against the wall.
``You're having too much fun!!``
Shion darted behind Takano, and pale blue sparks crackled to life. She had deployed her trump card -- the stun gun -- right into Takano's back!
No matter how strong you are, no one can withstand an electric shock.
Yet Takano simply turned around with perfect calm...
``My, my. ...What is that?``
``Th-that's impossible...!!``
``To think a mere child would dare do such a thing to the priestess of a god... What a naughty girl...``
Takano's hand closed around Shion's face.
Shion groaned and fought back, but the sound of her skull creaking under the pressure was audible -- her resistance was as feeble as an infant's against Takano's grip.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Heehee. This is your punishment, okay?``
The next instant, Shion's body was slammed into the wall.

changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Her body slid down the wall and crumpled to the floor with a groan.

``Stop it, Takano-san! I don't want to shoot you!``
``Oh my, Jirou-san. ...Heehee, what an interesting proposition. I'm genuinely curious to find out what happens if a bullet hits me right now...``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Takano gazed at Tomitake's submachine gun with undisguised amusement.

changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: She was genuinely smiling, eager to find out what would happen if she were shot.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: Tomitake couldn't comprehend it either. ...Right now, Takano had to be endowed with some form of supernatural protection.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``You made Takano your priestess, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto...!! How could you be so foolish?!``
``Silence! I do not take orders from the likes of you! I shall punish these new invaders who seek to defile our sacred land! Your interference is unwelcome!! Stand and face me, Une!!``
``...I h-haven't done anything...``
``Insolence! Know that your corruption is laid bare before my eyes!``
``...It was never... my intention to...``
``She said the exact same thing when I first arrived, and tormented me terribly. She's nothing but a bully!``
``Tamura-no-Whatever. I bear you no grudge personally, but I despise your priestess. So sorry, but I won't be holding back.``
``My priestess! ...The devil Une stands before you! Now is the moment to strike her down!!``
``...So that's where you are, Une-chan.``

Une sensed the gaze fixed upon her and trembled.
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``R-Run...!! We can't stop her!!``
Takano examined her right fist, then slowly pressed it into the wall.
Then... cracks began to spread through the wall, radiating outward from her fist...
``W-What's happening...?!!``
``Tomitake-san said run! So run!!``
``Une-chan, hurry, this way!!``
The cracks in the wall deepened before their eyes, and the ground itself seemed to quake.
There was no room left for doubt. Takano was going to smash right through the wall...!
``Why does Tamura's priestess have such incredible power, while your priestess gets absolutely nothing?!``
``Rika, don't say that! If you say it out loud, it becomes real! Auauau!!``
``Rika-chan, what are you doing?!! Run!! Uwaah!!``
A mere wall was no obstacle for Takano -- no, for the priestess of Tamurahime-no-Mikoto.
She smashed through it as easily as crushing a sandcastle.
``I found you, Une-chaaan! Heeheeheehee!!``
``Hii... Hiiii! ...Hiii...! Ah!``
``Une-chan!!!``
Une slipped and fell, unable to escape.
Takano wasn't foolish enough to let that opportunity pass. She strode right in and seized Une by the collar.
``Now I've got you...!!``
``Sorry! It was the pretty but oh-so-unfortunate Satoko-chan! Did you think I was Une-san?``
``Too bad for you! Ohhohohoho!!``
Takano, who had kept her composure even with a gun pointed at her, went blank for a moment.
After Rika shared Une's secret with the group, they had prepared the ultimate trap to protect her.
They had Satoko, who was close to Une in height, change into her clothes and swap places. Of course, the slip and fall to draw Takano's attention was also part of the plan.
The shock of seeing Takano's otherworldly strength smash through the wall couldn't overcome the thrill of a perfectly executed trap.
``S-Satokoooooooo!!``
``Ohhohohohoho! And here is a present for our dear fool!``
Satoko seized the moment of Takano's shock to place something on her head.
It was a... pair of white, fluffy@k@|@y bunny ears?!
``Now, Hanyuu...!!``
``Here I go! Flash!!``
``Huh? What's this...?! B-Bunny ears...?!``
Hanyuu had used her inhuman powers to interfere with Tamura's own power for a fleeting instant, creating a gap in the divine protection surrounding Takano.
Of course, since Tamura wielded enormous power derived from the vast lands she ruled, Hanyuu could only interfere for a split second.
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: However... that split second was more than enough...!
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: Rena's devastating blow connected squarely with Takano's face, and Keiichi swept her feet out in the opposite direction!
Takano's body spun through the air before slamming face-first into the mountain of rubble she herself had created.
Had Tamurahime-no-Mikoto's protection been active, Rena's attack would have been nothing more than the slap of a gentle breeze.
But in that one instant, she had reverted to the ordinary Takano. ...Which meant it was a devastating blow at full force.
Takano suffered a concussion and lost consciousness.
``W-What just happened...? Is Rena-san... really human?``
``...I... I'm stunned... Hahahahaha.``
Having just witnessed Takano's supernatural power moments ago, Shion and the others were beyond astonishment -- they could only manage strained smiles.
Of course -- they had just watched a club member instantly defeat a woman with superhuman strength.
``Uhh, don't go saying I did nothing, okay? This was my strategy. I devised it after careful consideration of everyone's strengths and abilities.``
``...Oh, Sis. You're here? I didn't notice.``
``I can't believe she took down that monstrous version of Takano-san... so effortlessly...``
``Une-san, there's nothing to fear anymore. You can come out now!``
``...It's not scary anymore...?``
Une timidly emerged from the shadows where she'd been hiding, dressed in Satoko's clothes.
...Under normal circumstances, simply swapping clothes wouldn't have fooled Tamurahime-no-Mikoto's eyes.
Takano had certainly been able to see Une through the wall using her divine sight.

But when she broke through the wall and confirmed it with her own human eyes, her human perception took priority, and she mistook Satoko in Une's clothes for the real thing. That's why she fell for the club members' trap.
``My priestess... How could you!``
``Takano has lost consciousness! You can't channel your power through her anymore!``
There's an amusing coincidence that the Japanese word for 'passing out' is written as 'losing god.'
Tamura may have been granting Takano tremendous strength as her priestess, but she couldn't give her an unbreakable body. Nor could she protect her from a concussion.
``How unfortunate for you. This isn't our first or second time dealing with Takano.``
``This is what happens when you underestimate the power of my priestess! Now, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto! You have oppressed us for a thousand years, but today, your tyranny ends!``
changeReason: Multiple significant content differences alter the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Hanyuu Flash!!``
``Nmwaaaaaaaaahh......!!!``
Hanyuu made scissor signs with both hands and pressed them to her forehead, and a brilliant light burst forth, erasing Tamurahime-no-Mikoto's image.
Apparently, erasing Tamura's image didn't mean defeating her outright. But Rika was grateful they could at least drive her away for now.
changeReason: Narration shifted from first-person to third-person ('I'm grateful' -> 'Rika was grateful').

TextENGNew: Tamura let out a scream and struck a pose like a defeated sentai villain before vanishing.
``Now, let's get Takano-san properly restrained while we have the chance.``
``She was incredibly strong! We'll need chains to bind her.``
Rika felt a pang of sympathy. Without Tamura's power, Takano was just an ordinary woman -- yet the restraints they used on her were absurdly heavy-duty.
changeReason: Narration shifted from first-person to third-person ('I felt sorry' -> 'Rika felt sympathy').

TextENGNew: Well, that settles that.
``...Is everything settled for us...?``
``It is! That awful nuisance Tamura is gone now!``
``Tomitake will handle the rest. Isn't that right?``
``Is that true, Mion-chan?! Amazing! You have everything here!``
``Well, there's nothing the Sonozakis can't get their hands on.``
changeReason: Minimal: 'we Sonozakis' -> 'the Sonozakis'. Same meaning.

TextENGNew: ``What's going on?``
``Apparently there's a powerful radio here! It sounds like if Tomitake-san uses it, we can contact the outside!``
It was the Sonozaki family's illegally modified radio.
According to Tomitake, it was capable of transmitting signals despite the radio interference.
``I'll test whether I can reach Tokyo. Une-chan might just be the savior we've been looking for!``
In order to weaponize a neuroparasite, a vaccine was essential.
The strength of Une's roots would serve as a potent vaccine against eta-173 and virtually all similar infections.
changeReason: Different numbers: old has set(), new has {'173'}.

TextENGNew: Tokyo would be very interested in that.
``That's amazing, Tomitake-san. You can do anything.``
``Well, hahaha... I-I just studied it a bit as a hobby...!``
``And with that, our grand adventure comes to an end... I suppose?``
``I hope so. I'm completely exhausted.``
``...Can I leave this city now...?``
``Yes, you can get out. You'll probably be kept under observation for a while, though. But they can't do anything cruel to you. Not with all of us watching.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation conveys different meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Une. Welcome to Earth. Let us bloom together on this planet. ...With Tamurahime-no-Mikoto's permission or without it. Au.``
``...I'm so happy... Thank you, Hanyuu...``
